research_list.name: GameStop
research_list.market_position: This trend is expected to persist through 2024 and 2025, with digital sales projected to capture an even larger share of the gaming industry's revenue.
research_list.future_outlook: With ~$4.2B in cash, GameStop can experiment with omnichannel strategies and new revenue streams; its success depends on turning physical advantages into a differentiated customer experience.
research_list.investment_potential: As of early 2026 GameStop holds roughly 10-12% of the U.S. video game retail market, down from a ~25% peak a decade ago, reflecting digital disruption in software sales.

research_list.name: SpaceX

Enterprise AI now appears to be entering this phase, as many of the world’s largest and most complex organizations are starting to use AI as core infrastructure. More than 1 million business customers now use OpenAI’s tools.

research_list.name: Intel Corporation
research_list.market_position: Intel builds and sells high-performance x86 CPUs and platform technologies for PCs, data centers, and AI workloads, combining integrated manufacturing (IDM) with design to deliver performance, security, and scale. By year-end 2025 Intel held roughly a 63% global share of the x86 CPU market, down from 80%+ a decade earlier
research_list.future_outlook: Intel's IDM 2.0 bet and 18A node push aim to reclaim manufacturing leadership while it also vies for AI accelerators and x86 market share.
research_list.investment_potential: TSMC supplies the bulk of sub-3nm chips for Apple, Qualcomm, and NVIDIA, maintaining a node lead that pressures Intel's manufacturing roadmap
